fresh_facts.claim: Approximately 2,200-2,500 US Marines aboard amphibious assault ships including USS Tripoli were deployed to the Middle East, redirected from Japan amid Strait of Hormuz tensions.
Over 50,000 US military personnel were deployed to the region, enabling 6,000+ combat flights.
US military strikes destroyed 100+ Iranian naval vessels.
Iran escalated missile and drone attacks on Persian Gulf shipping and energy infrastructure, including strikes across 12 countries.
Iran launched cluster bomb attacks on Tel Aviv targeting civilians.
Iran's actions effectively closed the Strait of Hormuz.
US conducted major strikes on Iran's Kharg Island, obliterating 90+ military targets.
President Trump warned of potential US strikes on Iranian oil infrastructure.
A US drone strike in northern Iraq killed a French soldier.
IRGC conducted strikes targeting US bases and the US Embassy helipad in Baghdad.
Iran rejected all negotiations with the US following Trump's latest provocation as of March 16, 2026.
Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi stated Tehran is prepared for resumption of talks with the US.
Nuclear talks between Iran and the US stalled after February 2026 rounds in Oman and Geneva.
Iran rejected US proposals on February 17 and 27, 2026 during nuclear negotiations.
A senior Iranian official on February 22, 2026 noted potential for an interim deal in early March while insisting on enrichment rights.
Hardline Iranian officials criticized President Masoud Pezeshkian's statements on Iranian attacks as of March 7, 2026.
President Pezeshkian expressed regret for Iranian assaults and noted instructions to avoid unprovoked attacks.
Iran's Assembly of Experts was expected to select a Supreme Leader successor as of March 7, 2026, with IRGC backing Khamenei's son amid resistance.
The US demanded sanctions relief from Iran in exchange for nuclear dismantling as of February 2026.
Iran was open to limited US contractor roles in oil and gas sectors but rejected US resource control.
Ukraine's President Zelensky discussed Iranian drone threats to Saudi infrastructure with Saudi Crown Prince Mohammad bin Salman on March 7, 2026.
US and Israeli forces conducted over 3,000 strikes targeting IRGC sites and missile facilities including Parchin, Shahroud, and Khojir since February 28, 2026.

regime_fall.current_status: Following Ali Khamenei's death on March 12 and Mojtaba Khamenei's succession as Supreme Leader, the Iranian regime faces unprecedented cascading pressure from sustained US-Israeli military operations (3,000+ strikes across IRGC sites, 100+ naval vessels destroyed, 90+ targets on Kharg Island, ~90% air defense degradation), ongoing internal divisions between hardline and moderate factions evidenced by Pezeshkian's dissent, complete Strait of Hormuz closure, economic disruption, and rejection of all negotiations despite prior diplomatic overtures.
regime_fall.last_major_event: US and Israeli forces conducted over 3,000 strikes targeting IRGC sites and missile facilities including Parchin, Shahroud, and Khojir since February 28, 2026, with major strikes on Kharg Island destroying 90+ military targets, while 50,000+ US military personnel and 2,200-2,500 Marines enabled sustained 6,000+ combat flights.
